md: This is a mixed practice set drawn from the kind of trigonometry questions you'll see on the **ACT** (and many other standardized tests). It's not organized by topic — instead, each question pulls from somewhere across the trigonometry unit, the way a real test would.

blocks.title: How to use this set
blocks.body: Try each question on your own first. If you get stuck, peek at one hint at a time — they're staged from gentle nudge to full setup. After answering, open the step-by-step solution to compare your reasoning. The diagrams in the steps are there to anchor the geometry visually.

md: **SOHCAHTOA & right triangles** — picking the right ratio from the sides you know.
**Unit circle & coterminal angles** — reducing angles, converting between degrees and radians, signs by quadrant.
**Trig identities** — Pythagorean ($\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$), reciprocal, quotient, angle subtraction.
**Graphs of trig functions** — reading amplitude, period, midline; identifying intersections.
**Law of Sines & Law of Cosines** — non-right triangles.
**Word problems** — angles of elevation and depression, ramps, real-world right triangles.

blocks.title: Strategy tips for trig multiple-choice
blocks.body: 1) **Always sketch.** If a question describes a triangle, draw it before reading the choices. 2) **Find the quadrant first** when signs matter — most ACT trig errors come from missed signs. 3) **Period before amplitude** when reading a graph — period nails down $B$, the trickiest constant. 4) **Plug in if stuck** — sometimes substituting $\theta = 30°$ or $x = 0$ rules out most choices fast.

md: Open the quiz below and work through all 17 problems. Each problem links back to the underlying lesson via topic hints if you need a refresher.
